text:
They pretty much do though during some filibusters. Seriously, when certain politicians decided to change what the filibuster was meant for (drawing out actual debate until resolution could be found), turning it into stalling for time instead... IIRC an infamous example was when the Equal Rights Amendment being debated in the '60s, & a right-wing politician against it stood up there & read all the entries in a phone book for hours & hours!

As far as I know, they'll do crazy stuff like that to this day. Talk in nonsense blabber, read bedtime stories or poems, etc. Just to try & tire out opponents & make them leave before a vote is finally allowed to be held.

All the more reason we should try to get rid of the filibuster (or return it to its original intent). Or at very least televise it so everyone can laugh & cry at seeing the true colors of who they voted for (Sometimes you can catch them at it on NPR as of a decade or so ago, not sure about nowadays though).
